response_raw:
{
"mode": "human",
"topic": "How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.",
"opinion": "To manage and deal with fake content on social media, it is important to have a critical approach when consuming information. Here are some tips that can help: 

1️⃣ Be cautious of sensational or inflammatory headlines, and verify the accuracy of the information before sharing or reacting. 
2️⃣ Use fact-checking websites and sources such as Snopes, FactCheck.org, or PolitiFact to debunk false claims. 
3️⃣ Be wary of content that is too good (or bad) to be true, and verify the source of the information before sharing or engaging with it. 
4️⃣ Use privacy settings on social media platforms to control who can see your content and information. 
5️⃣ Follow reputable news sources and journalists on social media to stay informed and get accurate information. 
6️⃣ Use technology such as browser extensions or mobile apps that help identify and flag fake content. 
7️⃣ Be mindful of your own biases and try to seek out diverse perspectives to avoid echo chambers and misinformation. 
8️⃣ Educate yourself on media literacy and critical thinking skills to better evaluate the information you encounter online. 
9️⃣ Encourage others to do the same by sharing factual information and promoting media literacy. 
10️⃣ Support organizations that promote high-quality journalism and fact-based reporting.",
"reasoning_summary": "To manage and deal with fake content on social media, it is important to have a critical approach when consuming information. response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.
response_parsed.opinion:
To manage and deal with fake content on social media, it is important to have a critical approach when consuming information. Here are some tips that can help: 

1️⃣ Be cautious of sensational or inflammatory headlines, and verify the accuracy of the information before sharing or reacting. 
2️⃣ Use fact-checking websites and sources such as Snopes, FactCheck.org, or PolitiFact to debunk false claims. 
3️⃣ Be wary of content that is too good (or bad) to be true, and verify the source of the information before sharing or engaging with it. 
4️⃣ Use privacy settings on social media platforms to control who can see your content and information. 
5️⃣ Follow reputable news sources and journalists on social media to stay informed and get accurate information. 
6️⃣ Use technology such as browser extensions or mobile apps that help identify and flag fake content. 
7️⃣ Be mindful of your own biases and try to seek out diverse perspectives to avoid echo chambers and misinformation. 
8️⃣ Educate yourself on media literacy and critical thinking skills to better evaluate the information you encounter online. 
9️⃣ Encourage others to do the same by sharing factual information and promoting media literacy. 
10️⃣ Support organizations that promote high-quality journalism and fact-based reporting.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary: To manage and deal with fake content on social media, it is important to have a critical approach when consuming information. Here are some tips that can help: 1️⃣ Be cautious of sensational or inflammatory headlines, and verify the accuracy of the information before sharing or reacting. 2️⃣ Use fact-checking websites and sources such as Snopes, FactCheck.org, or PolitiFact to debunk false claims. 3️⃣ Be wary of content that is too good (or bad) to be true, and verify the source of the information before sharing or engaging with it. 4️⃣ Use privacy settings on social media platforms to control who can see your content and information. 5️⃣ Follow reputable news sources and journalists on social media to stay informed and get accurate information. 6️⃣ Use technology such as browser extensions or mobile apps that help identify and flag fake content. 7️⃣ Be mindful of your own biases and try to seek out diverse perspectives to avoid echo chambers and misinformation. 8️⃣ Educate yourself on media literacy and critical thinking skills to better evaluate the information you encounter online. 9️⃣ Encourage others to do the same by sharing factual information and promoting media literacy. 10️⃣ Support organizations that promote high-quality journalism and fact-based reporting.
